QUESTION_TEXT: Write the type of C++ tokens (keywords and user defined identifiers) from the following: (i) new (ii) While (iii) case (iv) Num_2
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) new - Keyword (ii) While - User defined Identifier (iii) case - Keyword (iv) Num_2 - User defined Identifier

QUESTION_TEXT: Anil typed C++ code with errors: (i) Function strlen should have prototype (ii) Undefined symbol cout (iii) Undefined symbol endl. Write the names of the header files needed for successful compilation of the code using strlen and cout.
ANSWER_TEXT: string.h and iostream.h (or fstream.h or iomanip.h)

QUESTION_TEXT: Rewrite the following C++ code after removing any/all syntactical errors: void main(){ cout<<'Enter an Alphabet:'; cin>>CH; switch(CH) case 'A' cout<<'Ant'; Break; case 'B' cout<<'Bear'; Break; }
ANSWER_TEXT: Errors: (1) char CH; missing declaration (2) Missing { } braces for switch block (3) Missing colons after case 'A' and case 'B' (4) Break should be break (lowercase)

QUESTION_TEXT: Find and write the output of the following C++ program code using #define Diff(N1,N2) and array NUM[]={10,23,14,54,32} with loop from CNT=4 to CNT>0.
Find and write the output of the following C++ program code using pointer *Point and Score[]={100,95,150,75,65,120} with modulo operations.
Look at the C++ code using random(3) and random(4) with a 3x3 DOCK array. Find the possible outputs and maximum values of N and M.
ANSWER_TEXT: Correct Options: (ii) and (iii). Maximum value of N = 2, Maximum value of M = 3

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between protected and private members of a class in context of OOP. Also give a suitable example illustrating accessibility/non-accessibility of each using a class and object in C++.
ANSWER_TEXT: private: Implicit visibility, not accessible to derived class member functions. protected: Explicit visibility, accessible to derived class member functions. Example: class A { int X; protected: int Y; public: void Z(); };

QUESTION_TEXT: Which Object Oriented Programming feature is illustrated by Member Function 1 (default constructor) and Member Function 2 (copy constructor) together in the class TEST?
ANSWER_TEXT: Polymorphism OR Constructor Overloading OR Function Overloading

QUESTION_TEXT: Write Statement 1 and Statement 2 to execute Member Function 1 (default constructor) and Member Function 2 (copy constructor) of class TEST respectively.
ANSWER_TEXT: Statement 1: TEST T1; (executes default constructor) Statement 2: TEST T2(T1); or TEST T2=T1; (executes copy constructor)

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the definition of a class BOX in C++ with private members BoxNumber (int), Side (float), Area (float), ExecArea() and public members GetBox() and ShowBox().
ANSWER_TEXT: class BOX { int BoxNumber; float Side; float Area; void ExecArea(){ Area=Side*Side; } public: void GetBox(); void ShowBox(); }; void BOX::GetBox(){ cin>>BoxNumber>>Side; ExecArea(); } void BOX::ShowBox(){ cout<<BoxNumber<<' '<<Side<<' '<<Area<<endl; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Which type of Inheritance is illustrated in the given example with classes First, Second (private First), and Third (public Second)?
ANSWER_TEXT: Multilevel Inheritance

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the names of all the member functions which are directly accessible by the object T of class Third as declared in main().
ANSWER_TEXT: Enter2(), Display() of class Second; Enter3(), Display() of class Third

QUESTION_TEXT: Write Statement 2 to call function Display() of class Second from the object T of class Third.
ANSWER_TEXT: T.Second::Display();

QUESTION_TEXT: What will be the order of execution of the constructors when the object T of class Third is declared inside main()?
ANSWER_TEXT: First, Second, Third

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the definition of a function AddUp(int Arr[], int N) in C++ where all even positions (0,2,4,...) should be added with the next element and odd positions (1,3,5,...) should be incremented by 10.
ANSWER_TEXT: void AddUp(int Arr[], int N){ for(int i=0; i<N; i++){ if(i%2==0) Arr[i]=Arr[i]+Arr[i+1]; else Arr[i]=Arr[i]+10; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a definition for function SUMMIDCOL(int MATRIX[][10], int N, int M) in C++ to find the sum of the middle column's elements of the MATRIX.
ANSWER_TEXT: void SUMMIDCOL(int MATRIX[][10],int N,int M){ int mid=M/2; int sum=0; for(int i=0;i<N;i++){ sum=sum+MATRIX[i][mid]; } cout<<'Sum of Middle Column'<<sum; }

QUESTION_TEXT: ARR[15][20] is stored in row major order with each element occupying 4 bytes. Find the address of ARR[5][15] if ARR[10][5] is at memory location 35000.
Write the definition of a member function PUSHGIFT() for a class STACK in C++ to add a GIFT in a dynamically allocated stack.
ANSWER_TEXT: void STACK::PUSHGIFT(){ GIFT *T = new GIFT; cin>>T->GCODE; gets(T->GDESC); T->Link = TOP; TOP = T; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Convert the following Infix expression to its equivalent Postfix expression showing stack contents: X - ( Y + Z ) / U * V
ANSWER_TEXT: Postfix: XYZ+U/V*-

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a function definition for PURETEXT() in C++ to display the corrected version of file MYNOTES.TXT with all 'K' displayed as 'C'.
ANSWER_TEXT: void PURETEXT(){ char ch; ifstream F('MYNOTES.TXT'); while(F.get(ch)){ if(ch=='K') ch='C'; cout<<ch; } F.close(); }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a definition for function COUNTPICS() in C++ to read binary file PHOTOS.DAT and display total number of PHOTOS of type PORTRAIT.
ANSWER_TEXT: void COUNTPICS(){ ifstream F; F.open('PHOTOS.DAT',ios::binary); int count=0; PHOTOS obj; while(F.read((char*)&obj,sizeof(obj))){ if(strcmp(obj.GETPTYPE(),'PORTRAIT')==0) count++; } cout<<'Number of PORTRAIT photos :'<<count; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of C++ code that opens CLIENTS.DAT (200 clients), seeks to position 6*sizeof(C), reads a record, and outputs File.tellg()/sizeof(C)+1 and File.tellg()/sizeof(C).
ANSWER_TEXT: Client Number 8 of 200

QUESTION_TEXT: Which of the following can be used as valid variable identifier(s) in Python? (i) 4thSum (ii) Total (iii) Number# (iv) _Data
ANSWER_TEXT: Valid identifiers: (ii) Total and (iv) _Data. 4thSum starts with digit and Number# has special character - both invalid.

QUESTION_TEXT: Name the Python Library modules which need to be imported to invoke: (i) floor() (ii) randint()
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) floor() - math module (ii) randint() - random module

QUESTION_TEXT: Rewrite the following Python code after removing all syntax errors: STRING=""WELCOME NOTE"" for S in range[0,8]: print STRING(S) print S+STRING
ANSWER_TEXT: STRING='WELCOME' NOTE='' for S in range(0,8): print STRING[S] print S,STRING. Also range(0,8) gives runtime error; should be range(0,7)

QUESTION_TEXT: Find and write the output: TXT=[20,50,30,40], CNT=3, TOTAL=0, loop C in [7,5,4,6], T=TXT[CNT], TOTAL=float(T)+C, print TOTAL, CNT-=1
Find and write the output of Python code using class INVENTORY with __init__, Procure, Issue, Status methods and objects I1, I2, I3.
ANSWER_TEXT:
101 : Pad # 125
102 : U Clip # 150
105 : Thumb Pin # 35

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the possible output(s) from code using random.randint(1,3) and NAV list with loop. Also specify max and min values of NUM.
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) BACKRIGHT is the correct option. Max value of NUM=3, min value=1. OR: I or N not defined (NameError).

QUESTION_TEXT: List four characteristics of Object Oriented programming.
ANSWER_TEXT: Encapsulation, Data Hiding, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism (any four)

QUESTION_TEXT: In class Exam, both __init__ and Assign have similar definitions. How are they different in execution?
ANSWER_TEXT: __init__ is the constructor executed automatically when object is created. Assign is a regular member function that must be called explicitly to assign values.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write statements to execute function 1 (__init__) and function 2 (Assign) of class Exam.
ANSWER_TEXT: Function 1: E1=Exam(1,95). Function 2: E1.Assign(1,95)

QUESTION_TEXT: Define a class BOX in Python with instance attributes BoxID=101, Side=10, Area=0 and methods ExecArea(), NewBox(), ViewBox().
ANSWER_TEXT: class BOX: def __init__(self): self.BoxID=101; self.Side=10; self.Area=0 def ExecArea(self): self.Area=self.Side*self.Side def NewBox(self): self.BoxID=input('BoxID'); self.Side=input('Side'); self.ExecArea() def ViewBox(self): print self.BoxID; print self.Side; print self.Area

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between static and dynamic binding in Python. Give suitable examples of each.
ANSWER_TEXT: Static Binding: links function call to definition during compilation. Dynamic Binding: links function at runtime; code of function is unknown until executed, making programs more flexible.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write two methods in Python using Function Overloading (Polymorphism): (i) Area using Radius for circle (ii) Area using Base and Height for right-angled triangle.
ANSWER_TEXT: def Area(R): print 3.14*R*R. def Area(B,H): print 0.5*B*H. Note: Python does not support function overloading; second definition overrides the first.

QUESTION_TEXT: Show the status of list [52,42,-10,60,90,20] after First, Second and Third pass of bubble sort in ascending order.
Write definition of method EvenSum(NUMBERS) to add those values in the list which are at odd positions.
ANSWER_TEXT: def EvenSum(NUMBERS): n=len(NUMBERS); s=0; for i in range(n): if (i%2!=0): s=s+NUMBERS[i]; print(s)

QUESTION_TEXT: Write Addnew(Member) and Remove(Member) methods in Python to Add and Remove a Member from a List acting as Queue INSERT and DELETE operations.
ANSWER_TEXT: class queue: Member=[] def Addnew(self): a=input('enter member name:'); queue.Member.append(a) def Remove(self): if(queue.Member==[]): print 'Queue empty' else: print 'deleted:',queue.Member[0]; del queue.Member[0]

QUESTION_TEXT: Write definition of Method MSEARCH(STATES) to display all state names from a list which are starting with alphabet M.
ANSWER_TEXT: def MSEARCH(STATES): for i in STATES: if i[0]=='M': print i

QUESTION_TEXT: Evaluate the following Postfix notation of expression: 4,2,*,22,5,6,+,/,-
Differentiate between file modes r+ and rb+ with respect to Python.
ANSWER_TEXT: r+: Opens file for both reading and writing; pointer at beginning. rb+: Opens file for both reading and writing in binary format; pointer at beginning.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a method in Python to read lines from text file MYNOTES.TXT and display those lines starting with alphabet K.
ANSWER_TEXT: def display(): file=open('MYNOTES.TXT','r'); line=file.readline(); while line: if line[0]=='K': print line; line=file.readline(); file.close()

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a method in Python to search and display content in pickled file FACTORY.DAT where FCTID matches value 105.
ANSWER_TEXT: import pickle def ques4c(): file=open('FACTORY.DAT','rb'); try: while True: f=pickle.load(file); if f.FCTID==105: f.Display() except EOFError: pass; file.close()

QUESTION_TEXT: Observe the table MEMBER and write the name of the RDBMS operation used to produce RESULT showing only M002/ADITYA/HUMANITIES. Also find Degree and Cardinality of RESULT.
ANSWER_TEXT: Operation: SELECTION. Degree=3 (number of columns). Cardinality=1 (number of rows).

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to display all details from table MEMBER in descending order of ISSUEDATE.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM MEMBER ORDER BY ISSUEDATE DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to display the DCODE and DTITLE of all Folk Type DVDs from the table DVD.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DCODE,DTITLE FROM DVD WHERE DTYPE='Folk';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to display the DTYPE and number of DVDs in each DTYPE from the table DVD.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT COUNT(*),DTYPE FROM DVD GROUP BY DTYPE;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to display all NAME and ISSUEDATE of members who have DVDs issued in the year 2017.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT NAME,ISSUEDATE FROM MEMBER WHERE ISSUEDATE>='2017-01-01' AND ISSUEDATE<='2017-12-31'; OR use BETWEEN or LIKE '2017%'

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of: SELECT MIN(ISSUEDATE) FROM MEMBER;
ANSWER_TEXT:
MIN(ISSUEDATE)
2016-12-13

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of: SELECT DISTINCT DTYPE FROM DVD;
ANSWER_TEXT: DISTINCT DTYPE: Folk, Classical, Rock

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of: SELECT D.DCODE,NAME,DTITLE FROM DVD D, MEMBER M WHERE D.DCODE=M.DCODE;
ANSWER_TEXT:
R102 AGAM SINGH A day in life
F102 ARTH JOSEPH Universal Soldier
C101 NISHA HANS The Planets

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of: SELECT DTITLE FROM DVD WHERE DTYPE NOT IN ('Folk','Classical');
ANSWER_TEXT: DTITLE: A day in life

QUESTION_TEXT: State DeMorgan's Laws of Boolean Algebra and verify them using truth table.
ANSWER_TEXT: Law 1: (A.B)'=A'+B'. Law 2: (A+B)'=A'.B'. Verified using truth tables showing both sides equal for all input combinations.

QUESTION_TEXT: Draw the Logic Circuit of the following Boolean Expression using only NOR Gates: (A+B).(C+D)
ANSWER_TEXT: Use NOR(A,B) to get (A+B)', NOR(C,D) to get (C+D)', then NOR of those two outputs gives NOR((A+B)',(C+D)') = (A+B).(C+D) by DeMorgan's. Circuit uses 3 NOR gates.

QUESTION_TEXT: Derive a Canonical POS expression for Boolean function G(X,Y,Z) from the given truth table where G=0 at minterms 0,1,3,6.
Reduce the Boolean expression E(U,V,Z,W)=Σ(2,3,6,8,9,10,11,12,13) to its simplest form using K-Map.
ANSWER_TEXT: E(U,V,Z,W) = UZ' + V'Z + U'ZW' (one solution). OR E(U,V,Z,W) = UZ' + V'Z + U'ZW'

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between communication using Optical Fiber and Ethernet Cable in context of wired medium of communication technologies.
ANSWER_TEXT: Optical Fibre: Very fast, expensive, immune to electromagnetic interference. Ethernet Cable: Slower than optical fiber, less expensive, prone to electromagnetic interference.

QUESTION_TEXT: Janish used a pen drive to copy files; computer started malfunctioning. Which option caused this: (i) Computer Virus (ii) Spam Mail (iii) Computer Bacteria (iv) Trojan Horse? Justify.
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) Computer Virus OR (iv) Trojan Horse. Justification: Pen drive containing virus/trojan corrupted system files causing abnormal functioning like restarts and stopping applications.

QUESTION_TEXT: Ms. Raveena Sen accessed Admin password and provided confidential data to CEO about network vulnerability. Which option defines her: (i) Hacker (ii) Cracker (iii) Operator (iv) Network Admin? Justify.
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) Hacker. Justification: A Hacker is a person who breaks into the network of an organization without malicious intent; she informed the CEO about vulnerability.

QUESTION_TEXT: Suggest the most appropriate block/location to house the SERVER in Chennai Office (Admin/Training/Accounts) for best connectivity. Justify.
ANSWER_TEXT: Training Block - Because it has the maximum number of computers (150 computers).

QUESTION_TEXT: Suggest the best wired medium and draw the cable layout (Block to Block) to efficiently connect various blocks within the Chennai office compound.
ANSWER_TEXT: Best wired medium: Optical Fibre OR CAT5/CAT6/CAT7/CAT8 OR Ethernet Cable. Cable layout connects all three blocks (Admin, Training, Accounts) in a suitable topology.

QUESTION_TEXT: Suggest a device/software and its placement that would provide data security for the entire network of the Chennai office.
ANSWER_TEXT: Firewall - placed with the server at the Training Block.

QUESTION_TEXT: Suggest a device and the protocol needed to provide wireless Internet access to all smartphone/laptop users in the Chennai office.
ANSWER_TEXT: Device: WiFi Router OR WiMax OR RF Router OR Wireless Modem. Protocol: WAP OR 802.11 OR TCP/IP OR 802.16
